<!--- For HTML Only --->


<!-- \require{newcommand} -->
\renewcommand*{\vec}[1]{\mathbf{#1}}
\providecommand{\trans}{^\mathsf{T}}
\providecommand*{\mat}[1]{\mathbf{#1}}
\providecommand*{\diag}[1]{\mathrm{diag}\, #1}
<!-- %\renewcommand*{\det}[1]{\mathrm{det} (#1)} -->
<!-- %\renewcommand*{\det}[1]{|#1|} -->
\renewcommand*{\det}[1]{\mathrm{det}(#1)}
\providecommand*{\rank}[1]{\mathrm{rank} (\mathbf{#1})}
\providecommand*{\trace}[1]{\mathrm{tr} (\mathbf{#1})}
\providecommand*{\dev}[1]{(#1 - \bar{#1})}
\providecommand*{\inv}[1]{\mat{#1}^{-1}}
\providecommand*{\half}[1]{\mat{#1}^{1/2}}
\providecommand*{\invhalf}[1]{\mat{#1}^{-1/2}}
\providecommand*{\nvec}[2]{{#1}_{1}, {#1}_{2},\ldots,{#1}_{#2}}
\providecommand*{\norm}[1]{\left \lVert #1 \right \rVert}
\providecommand{\Beta}{\boldsymbol{B}}
\providecommand{\Epsilon}{\boldsymbol{\Large\varepsilon}}
\providecommand{\period}{\:\: .}
\providecommand{\comma}{\:\: ,}
\providecommand{\given}{\, | \,}
\providecommand*{\Real}[1]{\mathbb{R}^{#1}}
\providecommand*{\degree}[1]{{#1}^{\circ}}
<!-- \providecommand{\sizedmat}[2]{\mathord{\mathop{\mat{#1}}\limits_{(#2)}}} -->
\providecommand{\sizedmat}[2]{\mathord{\mathop{\mat{#1}}\limits_{#2}}}

\renewcommand*{\H}{\mathbf{H}}               
\providecommand{\E}{\mathbf{E}}
\providecommand{\widebar}[1]{\overline{#1}}

\providecommand{\Var}{\mathsf{Var}}
\providecommand{\Cov}{\mathsf{Cov}}
\providecommand{\HO}{\mathcal{H}_0}

<!-- \newcommand*{\E}{\mathcal{E}} -->
\providecommand{\V}{\mathcal{V}}

<!-- Index generation -->
<!-- IMPORTANT: For PDF output, all \ix* macros are defined authoritatively in         -->
<!-- latex/preamble.tex using \newcommand (loaded before the document body).            -->
<!-- \providecommand here is a no-op for PDF — it only takes effect if the command      -->
<!-- is NOT already defined, e.g., for standalone testing or HTML rendering contexts.   -->
<!-- Keep these definitions in sync with preamble.tex.                                  -->
\providecommand{\IX}[1]{\index{#1}#1}
\providecommand{\ix}[1]{\index{#1}}
\providecommand{\ixmain}[1]{\index{#1|textbf}}

<!-- \ixon / \ixoff: open/close a page-range entry, e.g. \ixon{topic} ... \ixoff{topic} -->
\providecommand{\ixon}[1]{\index{#1|(}}
\providecommand{\ixoff}[1]{\index{#1|)}}

<!-- \ixp{pkg}: index an R package under "pkg" and "packages!pkg" (used by pkg() in R/common.R) -->
\providecommand{\ixp}[1]{\index{#1@\texttt{#1} package}\index{packages!#1@\texttt{#1}}}

<!-- \ixfunc{sort-key}{display}: index an R function; both args include "()"             -->
<!-- sort-key uses raw _ (makeindex sorts it); display uses \_ so LaTeX can typeset it  -->
<!-- e.g. \ixfunc{stat_ellipse()}{stat\_ellipse()} (used by func() in R/common.R)       -->
\providecommand{\ixfunc}[2]{\index{#1@\texttt{#2}}}

<!-- \ixd{name}: index a dataset under "name data" and "datasets!name"                  -->
<!-- (used by dataset() in R/common.R)                                                  -->
<!-- NOTE: previously used \index{data sets!#1} (with a space), which caused makeindex  -->
<!-- to create a separate "data sets" heading distinct from "datasets". Fixed 2026-04-07 -->
<!-- to use "datasets!" (no space) so all dataset entries merge under one heading.       -->
<!-- Single-line: parse-latex expands these macros during the PDF pandoc pass by calling -->
<!-- pandoc.read() on raw LaTeX blocks. Multi-line bodies with % at line-ends produce    -->
<!-- %.\footnote{} when a footnote follows, commenting out the footnote opener.          -->
\providecommand{\ixd}[1]{\index{#1@\texttt{#1} data}\index{datasets!#1@\texttt{#1}}}


<!-- % R stuff -->
\providecommand{\pkg}[1]{\textsf{#1}}
\providecommand{\Rpackage}[1]{\pkg{#1} package}
